# WAVS

> A framework for building decentralized services that perform off-chain computation with on-chain verification. WAVS (WASI Autonomous Verifiable Services) enables developers to create high-performance AVS services using WASM/WASI for computation and EigenLayer for security.

WAVS streamlines AVS development by providing:
- WASM/WASI runtime for near-native performance
- EigenLayer integration for security via restaking
- Dynamic deployment and updates
- Cross-chain compatibility

## Core Documentation

- [Overview](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/blob/v0.3.0/docs/overview.mdx): Core concepts and architecture
- [Technical Details](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/blob/v0.3.0/docs/how-it-works.mdx): In-depth platform mechanics
- [Design Guidelines](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/blob/v0.3.0/docs/design.mdx): Best practices and AVS design patterns
- [Benefits](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/blob/v0.3.0/docs/benefits.mdx): Platform advantages

## Service Components

### 1. Trigger Contract
- [Example Contract](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/blob/v0.3.0/src/contracts/WavsTrigger.sol): On-chain event trigger
- [Contract Guide](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/blob/v0.3.0/docs/how-it-works.mdx#triggers): Implementing triggers

### 2. WASI Component
- [Example Component](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/blob/v0.3.0/components/eth-price-oracle/src/lib.rs): Off-chain computation in Rust
- [Component Guide](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/blob/v0.3.0/docs/how-it-works.mdx#service-components): Building components

### 3. Submission Contract
- [Example Handler](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/blob/v0.3.0/src/contracts/WavsSubmit.sol): Result verification
- [Handler Guide](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/blob/v0.3.0/docs/how-it-works.mdx#submission): Implementing handlers

- [7. Oracle](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/blob/v0.3.0/docs/tutorial/7-prediction.mdx): Example oracle service

## Common Issues

### Mac ARM (M1/M2) Setup
- Install Rosetta: `softwareupdate --install-rosetta`
- Enable Rosetta in Docker Desktop: Settings -> General -> "Use Rosetta for x86_64/amd64 emulation"
- Configure networking:
  - Enable host networking in Docker Desktop, or
  - Install network connect: `brew install chipmk/tap/docker-mac-net-connect`

### Component Building
- If error "no registry configured": Run `wkg config --default-registry wa.dev`
- For consensus, ensure deterministic execution and data sources
- Test locally with `make wasi-exec` before deployment

## System Requirements

### Core
- Docker & Docker Compose
- Make & JQ
- Node.js v21+

### Rust Setup
```bash
curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh
rustup toolchain install stable
rustup target add wasm32-wasip2
```

### Cargo Components
```bash
cargo install cargo-binstall
cargo binstall cargo-component warg-cli wkg --locked
wkg config --default-registry wa.dev
```

## Quick Start

1. Create project:
```bash
forge init --template Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template my-service --branch v0.3.0
cd my-service
make setup
cp .env.example .env
```

2. Build and test:
```bash
forge build
make wasi-build
COIN_MARKET_CAP_ID=1 make wasi-exec  # Test component
```

3. Deploy:
```bash
make start-all  # Start local env
export SERVICE_MANAGER_ADDR=`make get-eigen-service-manager-from-deploy`
forge script ./script/Deploy.s.sol ${SERVICE_MANAGER_ADDR} --sig "run(string)" --rpc-url http://localhost:8545 --broadcast
TRIGGER_EVENT="NewTrigger(bytes)" make deploy-service
```

## Component Development

### Requirements
- Deterministic execution for consensus
- Immutable/deterministic data sources
- No component-local state
- Proper error handling

### Project Structure
```
my-service/
├── components/          # WASM service implementations
│   └── my-component/    # Your Rust component
├── src/
│   ├── contracts/      # Solidity contracts
│   └── interfaces/     # Contract interfaces
├── .env               # Environment configuration
└── Makefile          # Build automation
```

### Component Structure
```rust
mod trigger;  // Handle event data
pub mod bindings;  // WASI bindings

struct Component;
export!(Component with_types_in bindings);

impl Guest for Component {
    fn run(action: TriggerAction) -> Result<Option<Vec<u8>>, String> {
        // 1. Decode trigger data
        let (trigger_id, input_data, dest) = decode_trigger_event(action.data)?;

        // 2. Process input
        let result = process_input(input_data)?;

        // 3. Encode for blockchain
        Ok(Some(encode_trigger_output(trigger_id, &result)))
    }
}
```


## Environment Configuration

Key variables in `.env`:
```bash
# Core Settings
WAVS_DATA=".docker"           # Data directory path
WAVS_LOG_LEVEL="info"        # Logging verbosity

# Network Settings
RPC_URL="http://localhost:8545"  # Local Ethereum node
ANVIL_PRIVATE_KEY="0xac0974..." # Local development key

# Service Configuration
SERVICE_CONFIG='{              # Component settings
  "fuel_limit": 100000000,
  "max_gas": 5000000,
  "workflow_id": "default",
  "component_id": "default"
}'
```

## Example Code
```solidity
// Trigger Contract
contract WavsTrigger {
    event NewTrigger(bytes data);
    function requestData(bytes calldata _input) external {
        emit NewTrigger(_input);
    }
}

// Submission Contract
contract SimpleSubmit is IWavsServiceHandler {
    function handleSignedData(bytes calldata _data, bytes calldata _signature) external {
        _serviceManager.validate(_data, _signature);
        (bytes32 triggerId, bytes memory result) = decode_result(_data);
        _results[triggerId] = result;
    }
}
```

```rust
// WASI Component
impl Guest for Component {
    fn run(action: TriggerAction) -> Result<Option<Vec<u8>>, String> {
        let (trigger_id, input) = decode_trigger_event(action.data)?;
        let result = compute_result(input)?;
        Ok(Some(encode_result(trigger_id, result)))
    }
}
```
